text.title: Ollama Agent Integration — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that gate any application of the ollama-agent-integration methodology. Sources: Ollama documentation — OpenAI compatibility; LangChain Ollama integration docs; LlamaIndex Ollama LLM docs.

rule.statement: Use the OpenAI SDK with base_url="http://localhost:11434/v1" and api_key="ollama" to make existing OpenAI-based agent code work with local Ollama models without refactoring.
rule.rationale: Changing one parameter reuses all existing OpenAI SDK error handling, retry logic, and tool-calling code without duplication.

rule.statement: Implement a health check against /api/tags with a 2s timeout before agent startup; on failure fall back to cloud API rather than failing the whole agent pipeline.
rule.rationale: Ollama unavailability (server restart, OOM, cold boot) should degrade gracefully to cloud, not fail the agent's task entirely.

rule.statement: Add output validation (schema check, required field presence, value range) before any downstream action on local model output — local models hallucinate specialized facts at higher rates than cloud models.
rule.rationale: Unvalidated local model outputs in automated pipelines can cause incorrect data to propagate silently, with no human review.

rule.statement: Never use OLLAMA_MODEL=llama3.1 (untagged) in agent configuration — pin the exact tag (llama3.1:8b, llama3.1:70b) to prevent silent model swaps after ollama pull.

rationale.source: Anthropic agent design patterns (transposed to local)

rule.rationale: Untagged pulls may resolve to different quantizations across restarts, changing response quality or failing to load on constrained hardware.

rule.statement: Validate that the produced `code` matches the schema in `content/02-output-contract.xml` before handing off (rule r5).
rule.rationale: Mechanical validation prevents downstream consumers from receiving malformed artefacts; the validator script enforces this at PR time.
